author: @paulhelberg5269
text: Your light-hearted approach is inspiring in itself. I find that inspiring people are honest (even when admitting their failings). They are able to set aside their egos to help other achieve the goal that is desired. They establish trust by giving trust and respect to others. In these ways I see you as an inspiring person and find myself, at once, interested in your presentations. Thank you very much!

author: @purplegirl8036
text: I wish you the would have recommended this channel to me a while ago.

author: @neilwelch7273
text:
Hi Rob, more great content as usual 👍 I’m an engineer with an enquiring mind but  unfortunately not as educated as yourself. That said I live in hope lol. Your comment about your youngun having ideas that hadn’t occurred to you is something I cling to. I’ve spent the last 30 years on an off dabbling with magnets, gravity, solar, wind etc, also in the hope that I might find something that greater minds didn’t consider. One day maybe. Your vlogs remind me of the Christmas lectures but better 
Truly Inspirational, thank you.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: you're covering a lot of ground here. Inspiring people by itself is an art. I had a boss who really could. No matter how depressed you where when you entered the meeting everyone came out with their noses in the same direction and eager to get the job done. Talking to him afterwards i found that he was in reality far less positive than in the meeting. It was just his way of managing people. A good thing no doubt.

author: @colouroboros9993
text: You're a role model, Rob.

author: @johnzudans7724
text: Excellent and inspirational. I am currently teaching kids at risk how to weld. I am often challenged on how to inspire them to get excited about the welding skill and your lesson in this area will help me to improve. Thanks

author: @bartoszbarejko1585
text: How is your GRAPHENE ? IT is very easy to make it with floting graphite but i can not do it. .

author: @ianbutler1983
text:
Rob,
Great video, thanks. I think you missed a feature that you have that is inspiring. That thing is that you do not show any fear of failure. This was Thomas Edison's great advantage. If it does not work, you have a laugh, consider that you have learned something, and move on. You demonstrate this very well on your videos.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is a nice point mate - I suppose it is because I know I am going to fail - especially the first time! and you are right it really is about what you learnt from it - cheers

author: @thaddeuspawlicki4707
text: That is very wise advice Robert, thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: glad you liked it mate

author: @davidoneill2466
text:
ok ok ok…..
My last comment to you i called you a Genius……
And now i know your secret sauce……
You are a Chemist…..

In a recent video you mentioned that your brother is a Physists 
Well i have you beat there . Both  may older brother Terry and my younger brother Brendan are Physists and both have had long careers teaching college physics.  Growing up i was heavily influenced by my older brother.  However it was Chemistry that grabbed me….
Well at least until i turned 18 and was corrupted by financial gain. And pretty much quit science as a career path.
 When my second son was born i recognized HIS genius early on and followed him to his elementary school where the staff allowed me to teach Algebra, Physics & Chemistry to the children there.
Today my son Cory is the chief chemist at Apple with dozens of patents under his belt….

WHEW………
This last video you made on inspiration.  You are not just a genius…..
You are a SUPER GENIUS….
You have not heard the last from me………..
Thank you Robert.
I am DEFINITELY on your team

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: to be honest mate - chemistry rocks lol - and Cory is clearly a smart guy that's for sure and bless your heart mate

author: @MrTubeuser12
text: after seeing this video Rob, it's obvious that you definitely are inspirational, to many people i'm sure (me included), thanks for all your hard work and great videos :) very appreciated.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thank you mate and thank you for taking the time to say that

author: @David_Mash
text: If a refrigerator compressor takes electricity to make "cold" why can we not generate electricity without much modification? External combustion generator?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: remember the electricity doesn't exactly 'make cold' what it does is turn a crank - so the electricity is used to make something rotate and that rotation is used to mechanically 'make cold'

replies.author: @David_Mash
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering thermosiphon. Creating a T delta should make a thermosiphon flow of the refrigerant and thus mechanically move the compressor as an air engine and then generate electricity. Could that not work?

author: @RB-bl7vq
text:
Excellent advice, A possible topic could be Life Lessons and apply them to everyday situations.
I've found with a total degree of certainty, things always take longer, are more expensive, are more
difficult than first thought and never worth as much if you come to sell anything.
Only two guarantees in life, death and taxes, anything inbetween is a bonus.
BTW Nora Batty likes your hair today.....

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nicely put mat and isn't that th truth - and I combed it so what's not to like lol

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Enthusiasm is a passion to help, a passion to teach and a passion to inspire. As you said, trust is the hardest thing to build and the easiest thing to lose.
Your personality is a large part of the way you inspire and also the way you inspire and motivate others. I have a lot of respect for you mate, especially the way you protect others from the haters.
A why is it so educator 😂

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mat - but basically I think I behave like anyone would

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering you behave like others should, not would. Big difference unfortunately, the world needs more people like you.

author: @angelusmendez5084
text:
Great ideas, well appreciated! 👏
For me the most inspiring thing in what you do is how you show us we don't need "approval" from an academy to solve our problems.

That spark when you get out your cage of concepts, take a look at the world and explore all the creative things you can make with the most simple materials.

Thanks mate! ☕🍪

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Yes! Thank you!

author: @reubenlongworth3586
text: You're a man after my own heart, I'm sure you've been through and experienced  life in many ways, I but you sound like myself, apart from the confidence aspect,it would be a privilege to meet you, though I probably never will ,you are an inspiration and  the kind of human being I  wish there was more of in this world, X reuben.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I rally do appreciate that mate and who knows maybe we will meet one day

author: @stewartpalmer2456
text: Dr. Smith, you've inspired me since i started watching your edits on making batteries. Let me ask you a carbon conductive ink question. Would it work as a reciever if you painted a digital antenna pattern with it? or would it need some copper particles in it too?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hiya mate - it's Rob not Dr Smith - the ink does work as an antenna - I have mad a few as it happens

replies.author: @stewartpalmer2456
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Just shooting in the dark here, but, you missed an inference in the original question. What properties are changed in your ink if it's doped with copper particles? Just a thought. Or even a copper ink doped with Carbon?

author: @geofft3214
text: I'm a Marmite guy. lol 😆Awesome discussion and tips thanks.  Inspiring people is a challenge and an ongoing learning experience.   Another area that I find difficult is to teach students problem solving or critical thinking.  - just do it, give it a go! right!  But often they don't even know how to begin a project/problem - the problem of where to start.  Really interested to hear you talk on this? 👍

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I understand what you man mate - where to start and making a start different problems and  sometimes intractable - I will give it a go - cheers

author: @gaiustesla9324
text:
the brain is the most valued gift you could have and freeing the mind up will grant any opportunity you can make up.

The development of your own memories is absolutely nessicary to become a creator rather than a follower. Seeing which ideas fail, things we see as junk thoughts most often act as a shadow for the ideas that work and stand out. So only learning whats "right" doesnt leave you with a sense of why it actually works.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: absolutely mate

author: @rayg436
text:
Great job 
And you exude confidence. 
But I think you inspire us with your actions and willingness to try new things and new ways of doing things. Or even the times that you fail and try again in a different way .
Again Great job.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

Thank you so much for all the inspiration Robert!

author: @stubluesjhb1
text: To me you are an old 'school' educator.  You encourage by example to try, get your hand dirty, and give it a go.  Keep on doing what you are.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @barbufodor1186
text:
Here are some of the reasons I get inspired by you (and this is the image you project to me):
                                                                                                       I am doing something
                                                                                                       I do not care if I fail, actually to fail at something is as good as succeeding
                                                                                                       I do my research
                                                                                                       I care about humanity
                                                                                                       I am passionate about what I do
                                                                                                       I want you to learn something today
                                                                                                       I do not care if they say it is impossible, let me try
                                                                                                       Sorry, I thought is possible  

                                                                                                       There is something here "they" may have been missed
                                                                                                       I do not not know all
                                                                                                       I build on the shoulders of my predecessors 
                                                                                                       I (occasionally) have the confidence of an ignorant (these are your words Rob)
                                                                                                       I am open minded
                                                                                                       I cherish any feedback
                                                                                                       Any gains are secondary to the experience and goal   ............
                                                                                                        I started you on this, you should take it further if you are interested 
I could continue for some time but I will be accused of "brown nosing" .

replies.author: @rayg436
replies.text: Lol love it

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's funny mate - but a person doesn't really think much about these things - but every now and then it helps to think a little about yourself - thank you for helping me do that - cheers

author: @saiello2061
text: Largely spot on. People are different though, that has to be recognised otherwise it precludes any progress towards moving to closing those gaps. Recognising differences in people does not mean they are not of equal value and thats the important thing to remember.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I agree mate thank you for pointing that out

author: @nobye9716
text: I also think its really incredible that you do all this with easily accessible materials, science should be accessible to all and I believe you do your part to make that possible, pretty damn cool

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I absolutely agree with that mate science should be in the reach of everyone and with a little imagination I believe it is

author: @nobye9716
text: You definitely have inspired me, it seems like the world works really hard to make people dull, and not interested in physics or science in general! And actually, when im watching your videos, I feel very lucky that I can learn from someone so smart, it feels like I am sitting in a class done by a famous scientist or mathematician! Because people like Einstein and tesla and etc. They were just really smart, regular guys who were curious and experimented with all sorts of stuff, and im new to the channel so im not sure if the young kid my age is your son or an employee but if its your son then I think thats really, really cool! I am a 23 year old girl and your videos seem like something that would not interest my demographic, and I think that sucks, the powers that be should work harder to get kids interested in experimenting like this!

replies.author: @AndreaDingbatt
replies.text:
100%Agreed with you!!
Many, many years ago, I was the First girl to do my mechanics course!
Thank you so much for your being interested in the less gender specific kind of things!!
It is great to hear that Rob is inspiring you, and from the Teen me, back in the 80s(,or there abouts!)
I'm glad to see you here, and I hope you continue to be inspired!!
Andrea. XX

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think you are right - the world drives us to the top 10 - the top 10 books, the top 10 films, the top 10 songs - etc etc etc - even the top 10 ideas and all that does is ensure the high street is full of macdonalds and Starbucks and everything begins to look the same - grey. To my mind colour comes from the individual working on their ideas and exploring what is interesting to them but it is hard to do that as the world tries to push you in a direction it thinks is right -it takes work to push back at that and yet it is essential to do so - cheers

author: @danrawson8515
text:
Absolutely Rob this works a treat to make someone's day.. a family friend used to say that to me when I was a kid.. so I stole that great idea and use it regularly... I often say hi to random people and I get funny results but generally it works well  acts of kindness are free,  and after all we are all connected, so we are one.. 
and at times like these we need to get more connected and build strong Community's...
